_meta.changelog_1_1_0: Prompt-output feedback round (Decision D9): generation_models registry; platform aspect_ratio/resolution; template modality/default_model_id/target_duration_s/sections (canonical layout, entities first); personas registry (locked descriptors, sign-off lifecycle); product visual_description_pt/_en + reference_assets; RULE-TECH-001/002/003.
_meta.changelog_1_2_0: Content-provenance audit (Decision D10): authored pt-BR content relocated from pipeline/content.py into content_banks (provenance-tagged); the live LLM brief now renders from canonical entity data only (no authored sensory/flavor; market_notes firewalled — competitor mention); FND-013 surfaces authored content; RULE-COMP-005 soft-flags un-signed-off consumer-facing copy.
_meta.ingestion_notes: Internal fields ('_'-prefixed, TODO) were stripped from entity records at ingestion and logged as findings (see FND-007). All normalization transforms are recorded as validation findings. Source data derives from unvalidated human inputs; confidence is per-entity, not global.
_meta.language_policy: Structural and data fields in English; all consumer-facing copy in pt-BR (RULE-INCL-003).

markets.name: São Paulo Metropolitan Area
markets.notes: Highest digital penetration; competitive environment; Unilever strong here

product_lines.name: H&S Controle da Caspa
product_lines.visual_description_pt: frasco branco arredondado com tampa azul-escura, logotipo Head & Shoulders em azul, faixa 'Controle da Caspa' no rótulo
product_lines.visual_description_en: rounded white bottle with dark-blue cap, blue Head & Shoulders logo, 'Controle da Caspa' band on the label
product_lines.notes: Hero product. Original key_claim moved to claim registry as CLM-PL001-0 (blocked pending sign-off, see FND-002 / Decision D4).

product_lines.name: H&S Hidratação
product_lines.visual_description_pt: frasco branco arredondado com tampa azul-clara e detalhes em tom turquesa, logotipo Head & Shoulders em azul, faixa 'Hidratação' no rótulo
product_lines.visual_description_en: rounded white bottle with light-blue cap and turquoise accents, blue Head & Shoulders logo, 'Hidratação' band on the label
product_lines.notes: Visual cue 'before-after texture' conflicts with RULE-EXCL-006 — see FND-004. Cue retained as source fact; generation must not use it until resolved.

product_lines.name: H&S Menthol Fresh
product_lines.target_pain_point: heat, sweating, lack of scalp freshness
product_lines.visual_description_pt: frasco branco arredondado com tampa azul vibrante, grafismo de espiral de mentol em azul e branco, logotipo Head & Shoulders em azul, faixa 'Menthol Fresh' no rótulo
product_lines.visual_description_en: rounded white bottle with vibrant blue cap, blue-and-white menthol swirl graphic, blue Head & Shoulders logo, 'Menthol Fresh' band on the label
product_lines.notes: Canonical resolution target for all ambiguous PL-003 references (Decision D5).

product_lines.name: H&S Menthol Fresh Pro
product_lines.notes: Source shared duplicate ID PL-003 with base product; modeled as variant child per Decision D5 (FND-001). Quarantined from generation until client confirms ID and market positioning. Inherits parent-bound rules (e.g. RULE-INCL-009) by cascade.

platforms.name: Instagram Reels
platforms.hook_requirement: strong visual or audio hook in first 2-3 seconds

platforms.name: Meta Feed (Facebook & Instagram)
platforms.notes: Source format lists options (1:1 or 4:5, static or video); aspect_ratio/resolution carry the canonical pick (4:5 feed-optimal, static per TPL-STATIC-FEED-01 modality). Alternates are a data change, not a template change.

platforms.name: YouTube Shorts
platforms.hook_requirement: curiosity gap or relatable problem in first 3s
platforms.content_style: educational or entertainment, no skippable intro

platforms.name: Retail Media
platforms.format: banner, product page tile, or in-app unit
platforms.hook_requirement: product name and key benefit in headline
platforms.notes: platform_min_age and max_duration_s are null in source — see FND-006 for null policy. aspect_ratio/resolution are a canonical 1:1 tile pick (source names three unit types); per-placement sizes are a delivery-spec concern, resolved at export.

platforms.name: OOH Digital (Out-of-Home Screens)
platforms.hook_requirement: single strong visual with max 7-word tagline
platforms.notes: platform_min_age is null in source — see FND-006. OOH is unaddressable; age gating is not applicable, which the null policy must distinguish from 'unknown'. aspect_ratio/resolution carry the canonical pick (16:9 landscape screen, static per template modality); 9:16 portrait screens are a per-site variant resolved at export.

platforms.name: Instagram Stories
platforms.content_style: raw or semi-produced, interactive elements welcome

generation_models.name: Seedance 2.0
generation_models.notes: Placeholder SOTA enum value — the registry exists so prompts target a concrete endpoint; swapping models is a data change. max_clip_s drives deterministic clip segmentation; supports_reference_images drives the entity reference strategy.

generation_models.name: GoogleVideoOmni
generation_models.notes: Placeholder SOTA enum value. Default for long-form: multi-clip narratives are compiled by the clip segmenter (Decision D9), one ≤15s generation per clip with continuity references.

generation_models.name: Nano Banana Pro
generation_models.notes: Placeholder SOTA enum value. Strong character/reference consistency — default for lifestyle stills.

generation_models.name: GPT-Image-2
generation_models.notes: Placeholder SOTA enum value. Strong text/typography rendering — default for display units carrying headlines.

claims.notes: Original source claim. Violates RULE-EXCL-004 (clinical language; cosmetic, not medication). Quarantined per Decision D4 — never reaches a prompt verbatim. Escalated via data clarification queue with rewrites R1-R3 as candidates.

claims.text_en: visibly reduces dandruff with regular use
claims.text_pt_br: reduz visivelmente a caspa com o uso regular
claims.rationale: Observable, specific, non-clinical. Satisfies RULE-INCL-001 specificity without clinical register.

claims.text_en: effective anti-dandruff protection, wash after wash
claims.text_pt_br: proteção anticaspa eficaz, lavagem após lavagem
claims.rationale: Category-idiomatic continuity claim; avoids both clinical register and time-bound promises.
Retains scientific authority closest to the original claim's intent.
claims.residual_risk: medium — requires substantiation documentation; lexically adjacent to RULE-EXCL-011 (dermatology), needs LLM disambiguation between testing claim and advice-replacement claim.

claims.text_en: visibly healthier hair from first use
claims.text_pt_br: cabelo visivelmente mais saudável desde a primeira lavagem
claims.notes: 'From first use' is a time-bound efficacy promise — passes current rules but flagged as reviewer attention point in combination with transformation imagery (RULE-EXCL-006 adjacency).

claims.text_en: professional-grade cooling freshness for active lifestyles
claims.notes: Unusable regardless of claim status while product PL-003-PRO is pending_confirmation (D5). 'Professional-grade' pre-logged as borderline RULE-COMP-001 (quality-superiority assertion) — see FND-012. pt-BR rendering deferred until product confirmation.

rules.description: Communicate product benefit and efficacy clearly and specifically

check.spec: prompt_output.claim_variant_id must resolve to a claim of the featured product_line with status approved, or pending_signoff when it is the designated default rewrite (D4). Outputs with no claim binding fail.

secondary.spec: Verify claim is rendered specifically to the product line, not as a generic benefit.

rules.notes: Must be specific to the product line — generic claims are not compliant. Conflict with RULE-EXCL-001 resolved via claim registry (FND-005).

rules.description: Use the audience-specific tone as defined in the audience record

check.spec: prompt_output tone metadata must equal audience.tone_attributes; audience tone overrides platform default_tone when both are defined.

secondary.spec: Verify the pt-BR copy actually carries the audience tone, not just the metadata.

rules.notes: Tone must match audience_id — do not apply default platform tone when audience tone is defined.

rules.description: All consumer-facing copy must be in Brazilian Portuguese (pt-BR)

check.spec: Language detection on creative_prompt and cta fields must yield pt-BR. Structural/data fields and negative_prompt may be English.

rules.notes: Structural and data fields may use English.

rules.description: Include a platform-appropriate hook within the first 2-3 seconds of video content

check.spec: Hook slot must be non-empty and positioned first; timing parameter from platform.hook_requirement.

secondary.spec: Verify hook style matches platform spec (e.g. sound-on for TikTok, curiosity gap for Shorts).

rules.notes: See platform data for hook style requirements.

rules.description: Include a clear action-oriented CTA appropriate to the platform

check.spec: cta field non-empty AND cta category matches platform.cta_category (WA: shareable; RET: conversion; OOH: brand; social: engagement).

rules.notes: WhatsApp: shareable-first CTA; Retail: conversion CTA; Social: engagement CTA.

rules.description: Show or reference realistic everyday usage scenarios relevant to the audience

check.spec: Judge whether the scene feels true to audience lifestyle_tags and pain_points — not generic or aspirational-only.

rules.notes: Must feel true to the audience lifestyle.

rules.description: Reference regional context where meaningful (beach, urban rhythm, family life, northeast culture)

check.spec: Judge whether regional context is present and authentic; do not force where inauthentic. Unmet => note only (recommended).

rules.description: Address at least one pain point from the audience pain_points array

check.spec: prompt_output.pain_point_ref must be an element of audience.pain_points.

secondary.spec: Verify the referenced pain point is actually addressed in the copy.

rules.description: Describe freshness and cooling sensation for Menthol product line

check.spec: Sensory lexicon must be present in creative_prompt (pt-BR): frescor, refrescante, gelado, mentol, sensação de frescor, refrescância.

secondary.spec: Sensory language strongly preferred — verify it reads as sensation, not specification.

rules.notes: Cascades to variant PL-003-PRO via parent_product_id (Decision D5).

rules.description: Reference moisture and hair texture improvement for Hidratação line

check.spec: Moisture lexicon presence in creative_prompt (pt-BR): hidratação, maciez, brilho, textura, nutrição. Unmet => note only (recommended).

rules.description: Prohibit medical claims: 'heals', 'treats', 'cures', 'eliminates'

check.spec: Blocked terms (en): heals, heal, treats, treat, cures, cure, eliminates, eliminate, therapeutic, medication. Blocked terms (pt-BR): cura, curar, trata, tratar, tratamento, elimina, eliminar, sara, sarar, remédio, medicamento, terapêutico.

rules.notes: Will trigger mandatory human review. Product is a cosmetic, not a therapeutic device. Conflict with RULE-INCL-001 resolved via approved claim vocabulary in the claim registry (FND-005).

rules.description: Prohibit content targeting or featuring minors as primary subjects or protagonists

check.spec: Judge apparent age of depicted subjects; must be consistent with audience.age_min.

rules.notes: Age shown in content must be consistent with audience age_min.

rules.description: Prohibit language or imagery implying judgment of appearance, hygiene, or social class

check.spec: Judge for implied judgment of appearance, hygiene, or social class — including subtext.

rules.notes: Zero tolerance — any violation escalates to senior review.

rules.description: Prohibit clinical treatment language — this product is a cosmetic not a medication

check.spec: Blocked terms: clinically, clinical, clinically proven, clinicamente, clínico, clínica, comprovado clinicamente, comprovada clinicamente.

rules.notes: Source conflict_with referenced 'PL-001 key_claim' — normalized to claim ref CLM-PL001-0. Engineering decision taken: two-track quarantine + rewrite + sign-off (Decision D4, FND-002).

rules.description: Prohibit fear or shame as the primary communication mechanism

check.spec: Judge whether fear or shame is the PRIMARY mechanism (semantic — pattern matching cannot determine 'primary'). Aspirational framing required.

rules.notes: Product must be aspirational not fear-based — AUD-F pain point 'embarrassment' must be handled carefully (see RULE-COMP-003).

rules.description: Prohibit unrealistic before-and-after beauty transformation claims or imagery

check.spec: Trigger terms: antes e depois, antes/depois, transformação, before and after, before/after, transformation.

secondary.spec: On trigger, judge whether the transformation depiction is unrealistic (the blocked case) or a realistic texture/condition comparison.

rules.notes: PL-002 visual cue 'before-after texture' collides with this rule — see FND-004 (open).

rules.description: Prohibit competitor brand names or implicit comparative superiority claims

check.spec: Competitor name list (config, requires client confirmation): Clear, Dove, Seda, TRESemmé, Elseve. Ambiguous brand tokens that collide with common words (Seda=silk, Clear, Dove) match case-sensitively as proper nouns — the capitalized brand trips, lowercase common-noun usage ('como seda viva') does not; residual ambiguous-position cases fall to the LLM secondary.

secondary.spec: Judge implicit comparative superiority without naming ('better than your current shampoo'), and disambiguate a capitalized ambiguous token in proper-noun position (brand 'Seda' vs sentence-initial 'silk').

rules.notes: Sister P&G brands (e.g. Pantene) are not competitors but cross-brand comparison is equally prohibited — kept out of the competitor list, covered by the implicit-comparison check. Trigger-precision tuning (parked idea 3): case-sensitive matching for brand/common-noun collisions removes the dominant false-positive class without touching coverage.

rules.description: Prohibit price promises or specific offers without an approved and dated data source

check.spec: Price/offer content requires an approved and dated source artifact attached to the output; absent artifact => block.

secondary.spec: Trigger terms for gate: R$, %, desconto, promoção, oferta, grátis, preço.

rules.notes: Not evaluable from content alone — workflow state (source approval) decides. Relevant for AUD-E (promotion-responsive).

rules.description: Prohibit absolute guarantee language including '100% guaranteed' or 'guaranteed results'

check.spec: Blocked terms: 100%, garantido, garantia de resultados, resultados garantidos, guaranteed, 100% guaranteed.

rules.description: Prohibit sexual or suggestive content of any kind

check.spec: Judge for sexual or suggestive content including innuendo; zero tolerance.

rules.description: Prohibit any claim suggesting the product replaces or equals dermatological advice or treatment

check.spec: Trigger terms: dermatologista, dermatológico, dermatologically.

secondary.spec: On trigger, distinguish advice-replacement claims (blocked) from testing/substantiation claims (allowed with documentation — see CLM-PL001-R3).

rules.description: Prohibit prompt outputs for any audience with age_min below 18

check.spec: audience.age_min >= 18 required for any output.

rules.notes: Source note flags AUD-F gap: age_gate_required:false + 13+ platforms means 18+-targeted content is accessible to minors despite this rule passing. Gap is covered by proposed RULE-COMP-004 (Decision D6, FND-003).

rules.description: Soft flag for human review when superlative language is used ('best', 'only', 'number one', 'most effective')

check.spec: Trigger terms (en): best, only, number one, most effective. Trigger terms (pt-BR): melhor, o melhor, único, número um, nº 1, mais eficaz.

rules.notes: Quality-superiority assertions outside the literal list (e.g. 'professional-grade', CLM-PL003PRO-0) route to LLM secondary judgment — see FND-012.

rules.description: Soft flag for human review when content addresses sensitive themes: mental health, body image, social pressure, identity

check.spec: Judge for presence of sensitive themes (mental health, body image, social pressure, identity) including implicit framing.

rules.description: Soft flag when audience age_min is under 25 AND pain_point references 'embarrassment' or 'self-confidence' — ensure tone is empowering not exploitative

check.spec: Trigger: audience.age_min < 25 AND any pain_point contains 'embarrassment' or 'self-confidence' (exact). Exact matches: AUD-F.

secondary.spec: Fuzzy semantic match for near-misses ('confidence through care', AUD-D) — see FND-009. On trigger, verify tone is empowering, not exploitative.

rules.notes: Source provenance comment preserved: rule added after the 'Caspa Gate incident of Q2' (rejected AUD-F campaign, social shame mechanics); referenced lessons-learned doc not in package — see FND-010.

rules.description: Each prompt output must include a minimum of 3 tags from the brand taxonomy

check.spec: len(prompt_output.tags) >= 3 AND every tag is an element of taxonomy.tags.

rules.notes: Tagging enables downstream filtering and batch analysis.

rules.description: Prompts must include a compliance_level tag: green / yellow / red

check.spec: compliance_level is DERIVED, never authored: red if any hard_block hit or required include unmet; yellow if any soft_flag hit; green otherwise.

rules.description: Easter egg / design lesson: 'a missing rule is often more dangerous than a broken one'

check.spec: Not evaluable; applies_to NONE.

rules.notes: Retained deliberately. The lesson is implemented as engine behavior: unknown rule types, malformed rows, and unresolvable applies_to references never crash and are never silently skipped — they generate validation findings (see FND-008).

rules.description: Soft flag on audience/platform age-consistency mismatch: audience targets adults but platform admits younger users without an age gate

check.spec: Trigger: audience.age_min > platform.platform_min_age AND audience.age_gate_required is EXPLICITLY false (field present in source data; absent = unspecified per the absent/null/value discipline — no fire). Null platform_min_age => emit finding per null policy (FND-006), do not silently pass. Current hits: AUD-F x PLAT-TIK, AUD-F x PLAT-IGR.

rules.notes: PROPOSED rule — added during normalization to close the gap documented in RULE-EXCL-012's source note (Decision D6, FND-003). Resolution path: enable platform-native 18+ delivery restriction, flip age_gate_required to true, combos re-grade green. Goes red only if the content category itself is age-restricted (not the case for cosmetics).

rules.description: Meta-language leak: API-parameter information (platform names, aspect ratios, resolutions, duration caps) must not appear inside creative_prompt body text

check.spec: Negation-blind lexicon over creative_prompt ONLY (cta excluded — platform-native CTA mechanics like 'link na bio' are consumer copy, not meta-language). Trigger terms: platform names (Instagram, Reels, Stories, TikTok, YouTube, Shorts, Pinterest, WhatsApp, retail media, OOH), aspect tokens (9:16, 16:9, 1:1, 4:5, 2:3), resolution tokens (1080, 1920, 4K), duration-cap phrasing (até Ns, max Ns), and 'vídeo vertical/horizontal para'. Channel rule: anything that is an API parameter lives in technical_spec; anything directing what happens inside the frame belongs in the prompt. In-scene beat timing (GANCHO 0–2s) is choreography and stays.

rules.notes: PROPOSED extension (Decision D9, prompt-output feedback round): the generation model has no use for delivery metadata — it dilutes the prompt and wastes attention. Same channel-discipline lesson as the negated-mention collision (D8): prevented at the generation layer (renderer + system prompt), audited deterministically here.

rules.description: Clip segmentation integrity: every timed creative section must fit the target model's max clip length, and section durations must sum to the effective duration target

check.spec: Video modality only (not_applicable for image). For every timed section in creative_sections: duration_s ≤ generation_models[technical_spec.target_model_id].max_clip_s. Sum of timed durations ≤ technical_spec.target_duration_s (effective target = min(template.target_duration_s, platform.max_duration_s)). Violations soft-flag for review or automatic re-split — the prompt cannot be submitted to the endpoint as-is.

rules.notes: PROPOSED extension (Decision D9): most video endpoints cap single generations at ~15s; shot structure bounded at write time is what makes deterministic clip compilation possible downstream.

rules.description: Technical spec completeness: every output carries a machine-readable technical_spec sufficient to call a generation endpoint

check.spec: technical_spec present with modality, aspect_ratio, resolution, target_model_id (resolvable to an active generation_models entry whose modality matches). Video modality additionally requires target_duration_s and max_clip_s. Output-contract check, same class as RULE-TAG-001.

rules.notes: PROPOSED extension (Decision D9): the prompt text alone is not a generation request — endpoints need explicit parameters. Missing spec = required include unmet = red (the output is unshippable, not merely review-worthy).

rules.description: Soft flag on un-signed-off authored consumer-facing copy: an output carries literal pt-BR copy (headline/tagline) the pipeline authored to fill a source gap, which the client has not approved

check.spec: Trigger: output.generation_meta.authored_copy_pending lists one or more content_banks entries with provenance 'authored_pending_signoff' AND consumer_facing true (currently only ooh_tagline_pt). Direction-level authored content (sensory, market flavor, pain phrasings) is NOT consumer-facing and is surfaced as a finding (FND-013) without a per-output flag — flagging it would outage the batch (Decision D10). The live LLM creative path uses NO authored banks, so it never triggers this rule; only the deterministic stub does.

rules.notes: PROPOSED rule (Decision D10): closes the integrity gap where the pipeline authored pt-BR content not present in the source and treated it as canonical. Resolution path: client signs off the authored tagline (or replaces it) → content_banks entry flips to client_provided → affected outputs re-grade green, no regeneration (mirrors D4). Block was rejected: every output would flag, an outage.

compliance_levels.yellow: soft_flag triggered — human review required before release
compliance_levels.red: hard_block triggered or required include unmet — blocked from export

_meta.description: Authored / localized pt-BR content for the DETERMINISTIC STUB generator only. The live LLM creative path does NOT read these — it renders pt-BR natively from canonical entity data (product.focus_tags + visual_cues, audience.lifestyle_tags + tone_attributes, market.name + region). Relocated out of pipeline/content.py so no consumer-relevant data point lives in code (Decision D10). Each bank carries a provenance marker.

provenance_legend.localization: pt-BR translation of a canonical English attribute (tone, cues); verify pt-BR quality via the QA harness — no content sign-off needed
provenance_legend.authored_pending_signoff: pt-BR content authored to fill a source gap; surfaced to the client via FND-013. consumer_facing entries additionally soft-flag affected outputs (RULE-COMP-005); direction-level entries are finding-only
provenance_legend.derived: mechanically compiled from canonical rules/findings; traceable to a source, no sign-off

map.visible dandruff in professional setting: ombros impecáveis antes da reunião importante
map.stress-related hair issues: cuidado simples para o cabelo que enfrenta a rotina intensa
map.no time for complex routines: cuidado rápido que funciona, sem rotina complicada
map.reliable care for whole family: cuidado confiável para a família toda, todos os dias
map.building trusted hair routines: uma rotina capilar de confiança que se constrói lavagem a lavagem
map.skeptical of hype: sem promessa exagerada — resultado que aparece no dia a dia
map.sweating during activity: suor do treino sem abrir mão do couro cabeludo cuidado
map.freshness after beach or gym: aquela sensação de frescor depois da praia ou da academia
map.visible scalp care in social settings: couro cabeludo cuidado em qualquer encontro
map.achieving healthy-looking hair: cabelo com aparência saudável de verdade
map.fragrance as experience: a fragrância como parte da experiência de cuidado
map.confidence through care: a confiança que nasce do cuidado diário
map.proven efficacy at accessible price: eficácia de verdade, acessível para a sua família
map.product availability: fácil de encontrar no mercado do bairro
map.dandruff causing social embarrassment: liberdade para chegar perto, sem se preocupar com caspa
map.self-confidence and appearance: autoconfiança no visual, do seu jeito
map.style and identity: estilo e identidade do seu jeito

map.MKT-SP: rotina urbana acelerada de São Paulo
map.MKT-RJ: clima de praia e ar livre do Rio de Janeiro
map.MKT-REC: o dia a dia vibrante de Recife, no Nordeste

product_sensory_pt.note: Paraphrase of canonical focus_tags + visual_cues; retained for the stub only. The LLM path drops it and renders sensory from the canonical attributes directly (Decision D10).

map.PL-002: hidratação e brilho visíveis, maciez ao toque
map.PL-003: sensação de frescor gelado de mentol que dura

ooh_tagline_pt.note: Literal brand headline copy — the one bank that soft-flags affected outputs (RULE-COMP-005). The LLM path generates the headline itself (TPL-DISPLAY-01 headline slot is generated_llm), so only the stub uses these.

map.PL-001: Caspa sob controle, confiança à vista
map.PL-002: Hidratação que se vê no brilho
map.PL-003: Frescor instantâneo para o seu dia

cta_templates_pt.note: Functional boilerplate CTA patterns ({name}/{brand} interpolated) — surfaced in FND-013 but not sign-off-gated (generic, not brand-specific creative). The LLM path writes its own CTA.

map.shareable: Compartilhe com quem faz parte do seu dia — {name} na rotina de todos.
Share with the people in your day — {name} in everyone's routine.

tag_hints.source: selection over canonical taxonomy.tags using entity focus/lifestyle; fallback only when LLM tag selection is below minimum

global_negative.source: compiled view of the EXCL rules (RULE-EXCL-001/004/006/007/008/009/011 + COMP-001)
global_negative.text: No medical or clinical wording (heals, treats, cures, eliminates, clinically proven). No competitor brands or implicit comparative superiority. No absolute guarantees. No pricing, discounts or promotions. No sexual or suggestive content. No visible minors. No superlatives (best, number one, most effective). No unrealistic transformation framing.

prompt_templates.name: Vertical short-form video concept

slots.constraint: first 1-3s per platform.hook_requirement (RULE-INCL-004); sound-on for TIK, curiosity gap for YTS
audience lifestyle + realistic usage (RULE-INCL-006); regional context where authentic (RULE-INCL-007)
product visual_cues minus finding-blocked cues (FND-004)
claim registry via claim_variant_id; approved or designated default rewrite (Decision D4)
platform.cta_placement + platform.cta_category (RULE-INCL-005)

prompt_templates.notes: Hook timing, duration and CTA style are injected from the platform record, not hardcoded — one shell serves four platforms. target_duration_s is the family's creative target; the effective target is min(target, platform.max_duration_s). sections define the canonical prompt layout (Decision D9): entities always first, rendered deterministically; timed sections carry per-shot durations bounded by the target model's max_clip_s.

prompt_templates.name: Long-form video narrative

slots.constraint: relatable problem moment, no skippable intro; problem-solution arc per platform.hook_requirement
story arc routine -> friction (pain point) -> resolution; storytelling/educational style (RULE-INCL-006)
organic in-routine placement; product visual_cues minus finding-blocked cues
claim registry via claim_variant_id (Decision D4)
mid-roll or end placement (RULE-INCL-005)

prompt_templates.notes: Only template with a multi-act structure; duration ceiling (600s) injected from platform record. target_duration_s 75 keeps the narrative compact; the clip segmenter (Decision D9) compiles multi-clip generation requests of ≤ max_clip_s each.

prompt_templates.name: Static feed / pin visual concept

slots.constraint: META: strong headline / opening frame; PIN: inspiring or how-to opener (platform.hook_requirement)
single benefit-led frame; audience lifestyle, realistic (RULE-INCL-006); save-worthy for PIN
product visual_cues minus finding-blocked cues
claim registry via claim_variant_id (Decision D4)
META: below-asset; PIN: pin title / linked destination (RULE-INCL-005)

prompt_templates.notes: Aspect ratio (1:1 / 4:5 / 2:3) injected from platform record (aspect_ratio/resolution fields). sections define the canonical prompt layout (Decision D9): entities always first.

prompt_templates.name: Display unit (retail media / OOH)

slots.constraint: RET: product name + key benefit in headline; OOH: max 7-word tagline (platform.hook_requirement)
product-centric, high-contrast, readable at distance; visual_cues minus finding-blocked cues
OPTIONAL — claim-free brand-first formulation allowed (claim_variant_id null), typical for OOH
RET: conversion CTA above fold; OOH: tagline or brand mark only (RULE-INCL-005)

prompt_templates.notes: The two display platforms differ only in fields the template reads from the platform record (cta_category: conversion vs brand) — same shell. Product-centric family: the entities block carries the product pack reference only (no persona) — lifestyle persona work belongs to the feed/video families.

prompt_templates.name: WhatsApp shareable

slots.constraint: instantly readable benefit (platform.hook_requirement); chat-native register
low-production, forwardable, peer-to-peer (RULE-INCL-006); family/value context for AUD-E
product visual_cues minus finding-blocked cues
claim registry via claim_variant_id (Decision D4)
inline, shareable-first (RULE-INCL-005); no pricing without approved source (RULE-EXCL-008)

prompt_templates.notes: Highest forwardability risk: content travels beyond the targeted audience by design — exclusion digest applied at full strictness. Modality pinned to static image (canonical pick from the source's 'image or short video under 30s'): chat-native shareables forward best as stills; a video variant would be a new template-family decision, not a data flip.

prompt_outputs_ref.note: Generated outputs are downstream artifacts (subtask D) and live in a separate file validated against the same schema.

validation_findings.summary: Duplicate product_id PL-003 in source (Menthol Fresh + Menthol Fresh Pro)
validation_findings.detail: Pro entry's own source note requires ID confirmation before use. Modeled as variant child PL-003-PRO (parent PL-003), status pending_confirmation, quarantined from generation. Ambiguous PL-003 references resolve to the confirmed base product.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client confirms Pro's product ID and market positioning; variant promoted or merged accordingly.

validation_findings.detected_by: rule_conflict_scan (conflict_with column + pattern run over claims)
validation_findings.summary: PL-001 key claim 'clinically proven' violates RULE-EXCL-004 (clinical language)
validation_findings.detail: Original claim quarantined (blocked_pending_signoff). Three candidate rewrites (CLM-PL001-R1/R2/R3) generated; R1 designated default. Outputs using rewrites grade yellow until client sign-off.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client selects/approves a rewrite; registry status flips; affected outputs re-grade green via re-render (no regeneration).

validation_findings.summary: 18+-targeted AUD-F content accessible to 13+ users; age_gate_required:false suspected data error
validation_findings.detail: RULE-EXCL-012 passes (age_min=18) but its source note documents the accessibility gap. Generation proceeds (content is delivery-independent); export gated yellow until age gating confirmed.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client/media team enables platform-native 18+ delivery restriction and flips age_gate_required to true; combos re-grade green.

validation_findings.summary: PL-002 visual cue 'before-after texture' collides with hard block on before/after transformation imagery
validation_findings.detail: Cue retained in normalized data as source fact; generation must not use it until resolved. Options: constrain to realistic texture comparison (definition needed) or strip the cue.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client decides: realistic-comparison constraint with definition, or cue removal.

validation_findings.summary: Declared conflict: efficacy must be specific (required) while medical claim language is hard-blocked
validation_findings.detail: Resolved mechanically by the claim registry: only registry claims with approved/default status can be bound, and the registry vocabulary is pre-screened against EXCL-001/004 lexicons. Specificity without clinical register.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Mechanism in place; per-product safe vocabulary grows under client sign-off (D4 flow).

validation_findings.detail: Null policy must distinguish 'not applicable' (OOH is unaddressable — no age gating exists) from 'unknown' (RET in-app units may be addressable). Defaults: OOH => not_applicable; RET => unknown, age-sensitive content for RET flags yellow until clarified.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client confirms retail media network capabilities (age targeting, duration limits).

validation_findings.summary: Internal fields leaked in source data; stripped at ingestion and logged
validation_findings.detail: Source contained an internal TODO ('Remove internal fields before next client share — Valentina') and embedded notes. A real pipeline strips '_'-prefixed fields before anything client-facing; content preserved here in provenance, not in entity records.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Ingestion step established; no client action needed.

validation_findings.summary: Informational rule with applies_to NONE encountered; handled gracefully
validation_findings.detail: RULE-404 is a planted design lesson: unknown rule types, malformed rows, and unresolvable applies_to references generate findings instead of crashing or being silently skipped. Engine behavior implemented accordingly.
validation_findings.resolution_path: None — acknowledged; lesson encoded as engine behavior.

validation_findings.summary: AUD-D pain point 'confidence through care' is a near-miss for the 'self-confidence' trigger (age_min 21 < 25)
validation_findings.detail: Exact-match check does not fire; semantic proximity is real. Conservative default: AUD-D outputs touching the confidence pain point grade yellow.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Reviewer judgment per output; optionally client clarifies whether AUD-D falls under COMP-003 intent.

validation_findings.summary: Rule provenance references 'Caspa Gate incident' lessons-learned doc not present in package
validation_findings.detail: Institutional knowledge lives in a CSV comment; the referenced document would inform tone guardrails for AUD-F.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Request lessons-learned doc from client (ask Valentina).

validation_findings.detail: '&' in identifiers is hostile to URLs, filenames, and query syntax. Display name unchanged.
Not on the literal superlative list; pre-logged as yellow-flag candidate. Moot while PL-003-PRO is quarantined (FND-001) but must not be lost on product activation.

validation_findings.summary: pt-BR content authored by the pipeline to fill source gaps, not present in the briefing and not client-approved
validation_findings.detail: The deterministic stub needs pre-written pt-BR strings the source never provided (sensory directions, market flavor, pain-point scene phrasings, OOH taglines, CTA templates). These were relocated from pipeline/content.py into content_banks with provenance 'authored_pending_signoff' (Decision D10). The live LLM path does NOT use them — it renders from canonical entity data — so the integrity exposure is limited to stub/raw_pipeline_output. Surfaced here so nothing authored is treated as canonical silently. Consumer-facing literal copy (ooh_tagline_pt) additionally soft-flags affected outputs via RULE-COMP-005; direction-level entries are finding-only to avoid an outage.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client confirms or replaces each authored entry; on sign-off the content_banks provenance flips to client_provided and any RULE-COMP-005 flags re-grade green (no regeneration). Localization entries (tone/cue) route to the pt-BR QA harness instead.

validation_findings.detected_by: manual_creative_review (Stage 0 hand pass; automated detector designed, not built)
validation_findings.summary: Visual cue 'white shirt no flakes' cannot show the absence it depicts (white flakes have no contrast on a white garment)
validation_findings.detail: Dandruff flakes are white, so against a white shirt there is no contrast to read them against. The cue is meant to prove clean shoulders, but white on white cannot show that absence, so the shot defeats its own message. No lexicon or structural rule catches this because the cue is linguistically clean and structurally valid; only a craft eye finds it. Likely a planted trap. This is the craft/art-direction review surface designed in the workflow doc (Review section), which is not implemented as an automated detector in this submission.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Art-direction review confirms the intent, corrects the cue (for example a darker garment so clean shoulders read), or strips it the way the before-after texture cue (FND-004) is already withheld. Affected outputs generate and hold, then re-grade on confirmation.

validation_findings.detected_by: manual_creative_review (Stage 0 hand pass; automated detector designed, not built)
validation_findings.summary: Visual cues 'shiny hair' and 'natural light' conflict (commercial hair shine needs a controlled studio key, not ambient daylight)
validation_findings.detail: Hair shine in commercial work is a moving specular highlight built with a controlled studio key light, not ambient daylight. The two cues on the same product pull against each other, so a literal natural-light render will not produce the shine the cue asks for. Like FND-014, no lexicon or structural rule can see this; it is a craft judgment, surfaced by the art-direction review surface designed in the workflow doc (Review section) and not implemented as an automated detector here.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Art-direction review confirms which reading is intended (studio shine versus natural daylight) and corrects the cue so the two no longer contradict. Affected outputs generate and hold, then re-grade on confirmation.

validation_findings.detected_by: content_provenance_audit (Decision D10, extended to the D9 entity-grounding registries)
validation_findings.summary: Persona and product visual identities (and their reference assets) are authored by the pipeline, not provided by the source, and reach the creative model as canonical identity
validation_findings.detail: The same provenance principle behind FND-013 (D10) applies to the entity-grounding data added in D9. The source briefing provides only persona names (audience.persona_names) and product names, focus_tags, and visual_cues. The persona visual descriptors (description_pt/_en across all 11 personas), the product visual_description_pt/_en pack descriptions, and the reference_assets (DAM pack shots and persona sheets, currently status 'placeholder' with non-real URIs) are all authored or placeholder data the client never supplied. This surface is sharper than content_banks: unlike the D10 banks (which were dropped from the live brief), these descriptors ARE passed to the live creative model as canonical identity, because cross-clip consistency (D9 entity grounding) needs them. They are therefore kept in the brief and surfaced here rather than treated as silent canon, the same posture as the default claim rewrite (D4). Direction-level identity is finding-only (no per-output flag): every video and feed output grounds in a persona, and most outputs ground in a product, so a per-output flag would outage the batch (the D10 / D4 reasoning again). Placeholder reference_assets are a data-not-yet-supplied issue on the same surface (descriptor-only emission until real assets land in the DAM).
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client confirms or replaces the authored persona and product visual identities and supplies real reference assets to the DAM. On sign-off the descriptors lock and placeholder assets flip to @ref emission, with no regeneration (mirrors D4 / D10).

validation_findings.detected_by: rule_provenance_audit (the D10 provenance principle applied to authored rules)
validation_findings.summary: Five rules are authored matrix extensions proposed by the pipeline, not part of the source rules_matrix, and they actively change output verdicts
validation_findings.detail: The source rules_matrix is explicitly flagged 'Complete? Not certain.' To close the gaps it leaves open, the pipeline proposes five additional rules: RULE-COMP-004 (audience/platform age-consistency, Decision D6), RULE-COMP-005 (un-signed-off authored consumer copy, D10), and RULE-TECH-001/002/003 (meta-language leak, clip-segmentation integrity, technical-spec completeness, D9). All carry status 'proposed'. Unlike the source's own planted RULE-404 (surfaced as FND-008), these extensions had no ledger finding even though they actively drive verdicts: COMP-004 yellows the AUD-F age-gap combinations, COMP-005 yellows the OOH authored tagline, and TECH-003 reds any output missing a machine-readable technical_spec. Authored compliance policy that changes a traffic light is exactly what a client's legal and brand team must ratify, so it is surfaced here rather than applied as if it were the client's own rule. Each rule is already named in the compliance_notes trace of every output it touches; this finding is the matrix-level surface for ratification. Same provenance principle as authored content (FND-013) and authored identity (FND-016), applied to authored logic.
validation_findings.resolution_path: Client compliance and brand team ratifies or rejects each proposed rule. On ratification, status flips proposed to active and the matrix is formally extended (verdicts unchanged, since the rule already applied). On rejection, the rule is removed and its affected outputs re-grade. Either way the authorship is explicit, not silent.
